title: Cigarette Smoking
description: Cigarette smoking is the leading preventable cause of disease, death, and disability in the United States. Smoking and secondhand smoke exposure cause more than 480,000 deaths each year in the United States. Smoking harms nearly every organ of the body and causes many diseases. Quitting smoking lowers the risk for early death and of developing smoking-related diseases.
source: CDC – Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

title: What Are the Physical Health Consequences of Tobacco Use?
description: Cigarette smoking has been linked to about 80 to 90 percent of all cases of lung cancer. Smoking also substantially increases the risk of heart disease, including stroke, heart attack, vascular disease, and aneurysm. Although nicotine itself does not cause cancer, at least 69 chemicals in tobacco smoke are carcinogenic.
source: NIDA – National Institute on Drug Abuse

title: Health Effects of Cigarettes: Cardiovascular Disease
description: Smoking is a major cause of cardiovascular disease (CVD) and is responsible for one in every four deaths from CVD. Estimates show that smoking increases the risk for coronary heart disease by 2 to 4 times and for stroke by 2 to 4 times compared to not smoking. Quitting smoking can protect people from cardiovascular disease and death.
source: CDC – Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

title: Alcohol Use and Your Health
description: Drinking alcohol can affect your health in many ways. Over time, excessive alcohol use can lead to the development of chronic diseases and other serious problems including high blood pressure, heart disease, stroke, liver disease, and digestive problems.
source: CDC – Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

title: Alcohol Use Disorder (Alcoholism)
description: Alcohol use disorder is when you can't stop drinking even when it puts your health and safety at risk. Treatment may include medication and therapy. Studies show most people with this condition recover, meaning they reduce how much they drink, or stop drinking altogether.

title: Alcohol: Weighing Risks and Benefits
description: The bottom line is that alcohol is potentially addictive, can cause intoxication, and contributes to health problems and preventable deaths. Any amount of drinking increases the risk of breast cancer and colorectal cancer. In general, a healthy diet and physical activity have much greater health benefits than alcohol.

title: Treatment for Alcohol Problems: Finding and Getting Help
description: Alcohol-related problems are among the most significant public health issues in the United States. Millions of adults have alcohol use disorder. This guide is written for individuals—and their family and friends—who are looking for options to address alcohol problems and understand what treatment choices are available.
source: NIAAA – National Institute on Alcohol Abuse and Alcoholism

title: Opioids
description: Opioids are a class of natural, semi-synthetic, and synthetic drugs that include both prescription medications and illegal drugs like heroin. Use of opioids, either by themselves or in combination with other drugs, is a major driver of the drug overdose crisis. The vast majority of overdose deaths involve illicitly manufactured fentanyl and other potent synthetic opioids.
source: NIDA – National Institute on Drug Abuse

title: Prescription Opioids DrugFacts
description: Prescription opioids are used mostly to treat moderate to severe pain, though some opioids can be used to treat coughing and diarrhea. Opioids can also make people feel very relaxed and 'high' — which is why they are sometimes used for non-medical reasons. This can be dangerous because opioids can be highly addictive, and overdoses and death are common.
source: NIDA – National Institute on Drug Abuse

title: Heroin Research Report: Overview
description: Heroin is a highly addictive opioid drug, and its use has repercussions that extend far beyond the individual user. The medical and social consequences of drug use—such as hepatitis, HIV/AIDS, fetal effects, crime, violence, and disruptions in family, workplace, and educational environments—have a devastating impact on society.
source: NIDA – National Institute on Drug Abuse

title: Cocaine
description: Cocaine is an addictive stimulant drug made from the leaves of the coca plant native to South America. Serious medical complications can occur with cocaine use, including cocaine use disorder and overdose. Also, adulteration of cocaine with highly potent fentanyl and related substances is a major contributor to rising drug overdose deaths.
source: NIDA – National Institute on Drug Abuse

title: Psychedelic and Dissociative Drugs (LSD, Psilocybin, MDMA, GHB)
description: Psychedelic drugs alter a person's perception of their surroundings, thoughts, and feelings. MDMA (ecstasy), LSD, psilocybin mushrooms, and GHB each carry short-term and long-term health risks. Adulteration of these drugs with fentanyl is increasingly contributing to overdose deaths.
source: NIDA – National Institute on Drug Abuse

title: Gambling Disorder (Gambling Addiction)
description: Gambling disorder is a mental health condition where betting takes over your life. It causes relationship, money and emotional problems. A major sign of problem gambling is when betting starts to harm important parts of your life. Treatment approaches include counseling, medication, and support groups.

title: Gambling
description: Gambling disorder is recognized by the WHO as a significant public health concern. This fact sheet outlines the health impacts, prevalence, risk factors, and available help for people affected by problem gambling.

title: Amphetamines Drug Fact Sheet
description: Amphetamines are stimulants that speed up the body's system. Some are legally prescribed and used to treat attention-deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD). Misuse can lead to addiction, cardiovascular problems, and psychosis.

title: MDMA (Ecstasy/Molly) DrugFacts
description: MDMA (3,4-methylenedioxy-methamphetamine), also called Molly or Ecstasy, is a lab-made synthetic drug that has effects similar to stimulants. It can increase energy and emotional closeness but also raises the risk of dehydration, overheating, heart problems, and long-term mood or memory issues.
source: NIDA – National Institute on Drug Abuse

title: Marijuana (Cannabis, Weed): What It Is, Side Effects & Risks
description: Marijuana is a mind-altering substance that comes from the Cannabis sativa plant. It has several short-term and long-term effects, many of which scientists are still studying. Short-term effects include altered senses, mood changes, impaired body movement, and difficulty with thinking and problem-solving.

title: Marijuana Research Report
description: Marijuana refers to the dried leaves, flowers, stems, and seeds from the Cannabis sativa or Cannabis indica plant. It contains the mind-altering chemical THC. People use marijuana for recreational and medical purposes. Research on its long-term effects on the brain, mental health, and addiction potential is ongoing.
source: NIDA – National Institute on Drug Abuse

title: Treatment
description: There are many options that have been successful in treating drug use disorders, including behavioral counseling, medication, medical devices, evaluation and treatment for co-occurring mental health issues, and long-term follow-up. No single treatment is right for everyone.
source: NIDA – National Institute on Drug Abuse

title: Tabak: Gesundheitliche Folgen des Rauchens
description: Rauchen schädigt fast jedes Organ des Körpers. Tabak ist eine der führenden vermeidbaren Ursachen für Krankheit und Tod. Die BZgA informiert über Risiken, Abhängigkeit und Wege zur Rauchentwöhnung.

title: Alkohol: Risiken und Folgen
description: Alkohol ist ein Zellgift und gehört zu den harten Drogen. Er kann körperlich stark abhängig machen und birgt große gesundheitliche Risiken. Eine Überdosis kann tödlich sein. Die BZgA informiert über Wirkungen, Gefahren und Hilfsangebote.

title: Cannabis: Wirkung, Risiken und Abhängigkeit
description: Cannabis ist die am häufigsten konsumierte illegale Droge in Deutschland. Der Wirkstoff THC beeinflusst das Gehirn und kann zu Abhängigkeit, Psychosen und kognitiven Beeinträchtigungen führen – besonders bei Jugendlichen. Informationen zu Wirkung, Risiken und Ausstiegshilfen.

title: Kokain: Wirkung & Folgen
description: Kokain ist ein stark stimulierendes Suchtmittel mit hohem Abhängigkeitspotenzial. Die Wirkung ist von kurzer Dauer, was häufiges Nachdosieren begünstigt. Konsumierende gehen damit erhebliche gesundheitliche Risiken ein – darunter Herzprobleme, psychische Störungen und Abhängigkeit.

title: Heroin (Opiate): Wirkung & Folgen
description: Ein Leben wie in Watte – so wird der Heroinrausch oft beschrieben. Doch die Droge macht schnell süchtig. Mit jedem Rausch riskieren Heroinabhängige eine Überdosis, die tödlich sein kann. Das Gesundheitsportal Österreich informiert über Wirkung, Risiken und Behandlung.

title: Harte und weiche Drogen: Was macht eine Droge gefährlich?
description: Die Techniker Krankenkasse erklärt, welche Kriterien die Gefährlichkeit von Drogen bestimmen – von Suchtpotenzial über Schädlichkeit bis zur Konsumform. Übersicht über legale und illegale Substanzen wie Alkohol, LSD, Amphetamine, Ecstasy und mehr.

title: Körperliche Folgen der Sucht
description: Sucht hat viele Ursachen und viele Formen. Ob Nikotin-, Alkohol- oder Drogensucht: Die körperlichen Folgen reichen von Lungen- und Lebererkrankungen über Herz-Kreislauf-Probleme bis zu Krebsrisiken. Die Techniker Krankenkasse informiert umfassend über Sucht und Behandlungswege.

title: Glücksspielsucht (Spielsucht): Ursachen, Symptome & Behandlung
description: Glücksspielsucht ist eine anerkannte psychische Erkrankung. Betroffene können nicht aufhören zu spielen, obwohl es schwerwiegende Konsequenzen für ihr Leben hat. Die BZgA informiert über Ursachen, Symptome, Risiken und Hilfsangebote.

title: Ecstasy / MDMA: Wirkung und Risiken
description: MDMA (Ecstasy, Molly) ist eine synthetische Droge mit stimulierenden und leicht halluzinogenen Eigenschaften. Zu den Risiken zählen Überhitzung, Herzrasen, Dehydrierung, psychische Störungen und bei chronischem Konsum dauerhafte Schäden an Serotonin-Neuronen.

title: Amphetamine: Wirkung, Risiken und Abhängigkeit
description: Amphetamine wie Speed oder Crystal Meth sind starke Stimulanzien. Sie steigern Wachheit und Leistungsfähigkeit, führen aber rasch zu Abhängigkeit, psychischen Störungen und körperlichem Verfall. Informationen zu Wirkung, Gefahren und Ausstiegsmöglichkeiten.

title: LSD: Wirkung, Risiken und Nebenwirkungen
description: LSD (Lysergsäurediethylamid) ist ein starkes Halluzinogen. Bereits kleine Mengen erzeugen intensive wahrnehmungsverändernde Erfahrungen. Risiken umfassen Horrortrips, Flashbacks, psychische Destabilisierung und mögliche Auslösung latenter psychischer Erkrankungen.

title: Pilze (Psilocybin): Wirkung und Risiken
description: Psilocybin-haltige Pilze sind natürliche Halluzinogene, die intensive wahrnehmungsverändernde Effekte auslösen können. Risiken bestehen vor allem in schlechten Trips, psychischer Destabilisierung und dem Risiko von Unfällen unter Einfluss.

title: GHB / GBL: Wirkung, Risiken und Abhängigkeit
description: GHB (Gamma-Hydroxybuttersäure) ist ein dämpfendes Suchtmittel, das in der Partyszene verbreitet ist. Der Dosierungsspielraum zwischen Rauschwirkung und lebensgefährlicher Überdosierung ist sehr gering. GHB wurde auch als K.o.-Tropfen missbraucht.

title: Tabagismo: o que é, causas, efeitos e tratamento
description: O tabagismo é reconhecido pela OMS como uma pandemia. No Brasil, são mais de 161 mil mortes anuais atribuíveis ao uso de tabaco. O Instituto Nacional do Câncer (INCA) classifica o tabagismo como doença crônica causada pela dependência à nicotina.

title: Álcool e outras drogas: informações e prevenção
description: O Ministério da Saúde brasileiro oferece um guia estratégico para o cuidado de pessoas com necessidades relacionadas ao consumo de álcool e outras drogas. Aborda dependência, tratamento, redução de danos e políticas públicas de saúde.

title: Drogas: informações para a prevenção
description: A Fiocruz (Fundação Oswaldo Cruz) disponibiliza informações científicas sobre substâncias psicoativas, seus efeitos, riscos e dependência. Aborda prevenção ao uso de drogas e estratégias de redução de danos.

title: Dependência de álcool: sintomas, causas e tratamento
description: A dependência de álcool é uma doença crônica que afeta milhões de brasileiros. O Ministério da Saúde orienta sobre os sinais de alerta, consequências para a saúde física e mental, e os caminhos para o tratamento e a recuperação.

title: Cannabis (maconha): efeitos, riscos e dependência
description: A cannabis é a droga ilícita mais consumida no Brasil. O THC, seu princípio ativo, atua no sistema nervoso central e pode causar dependência, comprometimento cognitivo e problemas de saúde mental, especialmente em usuários jovens.
source: SENAD – Secretaria Nacional de Políticas sobre Drogas

title: Jogo patológico (ludopatia): o que é e como tratar
description: O jogo patológico é um transtorno do controlo dos impulsos reconhecido como doença pelo DSM-5. Afeta finanças, relacionamentos e saúde mental. O tratamento inclui psicoterapia cognitivo-comportamental e grupos de apoio.

title: Tabaco: riesgos y consecuencias para la salud
description: El Plan Nacional sobre Drogas del Ministerio de Sanidad de España informa sobre los riesgos del tabaco: cáncer, enfermedades cardiovasculares y pulmonares. El tabaquismo es la principal causa evitable de enfermedad y muerte prematura en España, con más de 50.000 muertes anuales.

title: Alcohol: prevención del consumo
description: El alcohol es la sustancia psicoactiva más consumida en España. Contribuye al desarrollo de más de 200 problemas de salud y lesiones, así como a muerte prematura. No existe un límite de seguridad en el consumo por debajo del cual no exista riesgo de cáncer.

title: Cannabis: riesgos y efectos sobre la salud
description: El cannabis es la droga ilegal más consumida en España. Su consumo regular puede causar dependencia psicológica, trastornos mentales (psicosis, esquizofrenia), alteraciones cognitivas y problemas respiratorios. El Plan Nacional sobre Drogas ofrece información completa sobre sus riesgos.

title: Cocaína: riesgos, efectos y dependencia
description: La cocaína es un potente estimulante del sistema nervioso central con alto potencial adictivo. El Plan Nacional sobre Drogas informa sobre sus efectos, riesgos cardiovasculares, psiquiátricos y el riesgo de sobredosis, especialmente cuando se mezcla con otras sustancias.

title: Heroína: efectos, riesgos y tratamiento
description: La heroína es un opioide altamente adictivo derivado de la morfina. Su consumo conlleva riesgo de sobredosis mortal, enfermedades infecciosas como el VIH y la hepatitis, y graves consecuencias sociales. Se describe la evolución del consumo y los tratamientos de sustitución disponibles.

title: Éxtasis (MDMA): efectos, riesgos y dependencia
description: El éxtasis actúa como estimulante y alucinógeno. Aumenta la energía y la sociabilidad, pero expone a riesgos de deshidratación, golpe de calor, problemas cardíacos y daños neurológicos a largo plazo. El PNSD ofrece información científica actualizada sobre esta sustancia.

title: Anfetaminas y metanfetaminas: riesgos para la salud
description: Las anfetaminas son estimulantes potentes del sistema nervioso central. Su uso crónico puede provocar psicosis, desnutrición, problemas cardiovasculares graves y dependencia severa. El PNSD informa sobre tipos, efectos y vías de tratamiento.

title: Trastorno del juego (adicción al juego): qué es y cómo tratarlo
description: El trastorno del juego es reconocido por el DSM-5 como un trastorno adictivo. La Organización Mundial de la Salud subraya su impacto en la salud mental, las finanzas y las relaciones personales. El Plan Nacional sobre Drogas ofrece recursos de prevención y tratamiento.

title: Tabac : effets sur la santé, dépendance et arrêt
description: Le tabac est responsable de plus de 75 000 décès par an en France. Santé publique France informe sur les effets du tabagisme sur les poumons, le cœur et le risque de cancer, ainsi que sur les méthodes d'arrêt et les substituts nicotiniques.

title: Alcool : effets sur la santé et risques
description: L'alcool est un produit psychoactif qui a un effet direct sur le cerveau. À forte dose, il provoque l'ivresse et peut entraîner un coma éthylique. Sa consommation régulière augmente le risque de maladies cardiovasculaires, de cancers et de dépendance.

title: Qu'est-ce qu'une drogue ?
description: On appelle « drogue » toute substance qui modifie la manière de percevoir les choses, de ressentir les émotions, de penser et de se comporter. Il existe des drogues autorisées réglementées comme l'alcool et le tabac, et des drogues interdites comme le cannabis, l'héroïne ou la cocaïne.

title: Cannabis : effets, risques et dépendance
description: Malgré la baisse de son usage chez les mineurs, le cannabis reste la drogue illicite la plus consommée en France. Sa consommation régulière entraîne des troubles de la mémoire, une baisse de motivation, et peut favoriser des troubles psychotiques chez les jeunes.
source: MILDECA – Mission interministérielle de lutte contre les drogues et les conduites addictives

title: Drogues illicites : usages, risques et accompagnements
description: Le guide de référence de la MILDECA (décembre 2025) couvre toutes les drogues illicites : cannabis, cocaïne, héroïne, MDMA, LSD, champignons, GHB, amphétamines et opioïdes. Il présente leurs effets, risques sanitaires, potentiel addictif et les ressources d'accompagnement disponibles en France.
source: MILDECA – Mission interministérielle de lutte contre les drogues et les conduites addictives

title: Réduction des risques et des dommages liés aux drogues
description: La politique française de réduction des risques et des dommages (RDRD) permet de considérer l'addiction comme une maladie chronique. Elle couvre héroïne, cocaïne, crack et autres drogues injectées ou inhalées, avec des dispositifs comme les haltes soins addictions.
source: MILDECA – Mission interministérielle de lutte contre les drogues et les conduites addictives

title: Jeu excessif : reconnaître et traiter l'addiction au jeu
description: Le jeu problématique touche environ 1 million de joueurs « à risque modéré » et 200 000 joueurs « excessifs » en France. Santé publique France informe sur les signes d'une addiction au jeu, ses conséquences et les ressources disponibles.

title: Héroïne et opioïdes : effets, risques et traitement
description: L'héroïne et les autres opioïdes entraînent une dépendance rapide et sévère. Les risques incluent l'overdose mortelle, les infections liées au partage de matériel, et les maladies comme le VIH et l'hépatite C. Des traitements de substitution (méthadone, buprénorphine) sont disponibles en France.
source: MILDECA – Mission interministérielle de lutte contre les drogues et les conduites addictives

title: Facteurs de risque et facteurs de protection face aux addictions
description: La MILDECA analyse les facteurs qui favorisent ou protègent du développement d'une addiction : génétique, environnement familial, précocité de la consommation et compétences psychosociales. Commencer à boire de l'alcool au début de l'adolescence multiplie par dix le risque de devenir alcoolo-dépendant.
source: MILDECA – Mission interministérielle de lutte contre les drogues et les conduites addictives

title: Cannabis, cannabinoids and health: a review of evidence on risks and medical benefits
description: The legalization of cannabis for medical and recreational purposes has progressed internationally. Cannabis and cannabinoids are advocated for a plethora of medical indications. An increasing number of medical and nonmedical users regularly consume large doses of delta-9-Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), the main active component of cannabis.
author: Hoch, E., Volkow, N.D., Friemel, C.M. et al.

title: World Drug Report 2025
description: A global reference on drug markets, trends and policy developments, the World Drug Report offers a wealth of data and analysis and in 2025 comprises several elements tailored to different audiences.
